title: Devanagari model (headline/conjuncts/matras)
context: Devanagari is used by Hindi, Sanskrit, Nepali, Marathi, and many other South Asian languages, making it one of the world's most widely written scripts. It is underserved by free, correctly-formed practice materials. Devanagari has unique structural features — the headline (shirorekha), conjunct consonants (stacked forms), and matras (vowel diacritics) — that generic tools consistently mangle. This task is blocked on securing a script-literate reviewer who is a native/fluent Devanagari writer or qualified teacher.
objective: Model Devanagari consonants, vowels, matras, and common conjunct forms as schema-valid GlyphModel JSON with per-glyph sourceRef and headline/shirorekha guideline system, using a gate-verified open Devanagari font and with script-literate reviewer sign-off.
acceptanceCriteria: BLOCKER: A script-literate Devanagari reviewer (native/fluent writer or qualified teacher of Hindi/Sanskrit/Nepali or another Devanagari-script language) is named and confirmed.
Devanagari consonants (ka–ha), independent vowels, vowel matras, anusvara, visarga, and a representative set of common conjuncts modeled as schema-valid GlyphModel JSON with per-glyph sourceRef.
Guideline system includes the shirorekha (headline/header line) specific to Devanagari; guideline geometry parameterized in GuidelineSystem.
Gate-verified open Devanagari font sourced (gate-002 PASS artifact attached); SOURCES.md committed with provenance and snapshots.
Script-literate reviewer signs the per-glyph checklist confirming stroke order, conjunct forms, matra placement, and headline alignment match the authoritative source.
Sheets render in A4 and US Letter; CC-BY-4.0 with attribution; provenance-gate green.
resources: PLAN.md section 9 M4, section 2 (Indic scripts as underserved), section 5 (Devanagari with headline/conjuncts/shirorekha in guideline systems)
output: data/devanagari/ directory with schema-valid GlyphModel JSON files (consonants, vowels, matras, conjuncts), SOURCES.md, Devanagari guideline system, reviewer-signed checklist, and trace-sheet PDFs — committed CC-BY-4.0.
